name: SuperPawn
description_short: Phoenix, AZ's SuperPawn at 4234 W Northern Ave offers quick cash loans with flexible terms for personal items.
description_long:
SuperPawn's W Northern Avenue location in Phoenix, AZ sits in a high-traffic area of the city, serving residents from central and west Phoenix. The store is open Monday through Saturday from 9 AM to 7 PM, and Sunday from 12 PM to 5 PM, making it convenient for locals to stop by on their schedule.

This Phoenix SuperPawn specializes in short-term cash loans against jewelry, electronics, tools, and other personal items. The staff can evaluate your items and provide an instant quote. Call +1 623-934-5411 to ask about specific items or get started with a loan.

For Phoenix residents in need of quick cash, SuperPawn offers a faster alternative to traditional loans and credit. Bring a valid ID and the items you'd like to pawn — the location handles most common valuables and can often close a transaction in minutes.
pros: Non-recourse pawn loans mean borrowers cannot be pursued for deficiencies if pawned items sell for less than the loan amount
3,300+ locations across 29 U.S. states, UK, and Latin America provide convenient access and scale
Fast cash transactions—immediate payment for items sold and quick loan approval without credit checks
Wide variety of accepted collateral items including jewelry, electronics, tools, appliances, sporting goods, and instruments
Layaway service available with only 10% down payment for customers wanting to purchase items gradually
Gold and precious metals buying program offers immediate cash at market rates for a common household asset
Publicly-traded company (S&P MidCap 400 and Russell 2000 components) providing operational stability and transparency
cons: Pawn loans are a high-cost borrowing method compared to personal loans, payday alternatives, or traditional credit—interest rates not disclosed on website but historically 15-25% monthly
Risk of losing collateral entirely if borrowed amount is not repaid by loan maturity date
No credit-building benefit—pawn loans are not reported to credit bureaus, so borrowing does not improve credit scores
Limited to customers with physical items of sufficient value to pledge; may not work for those with no valuable assets
Retail merchandise quality and selection dependent on what others pawn, creating inconsistent inventory
best_for: Consumers with immediate cash needs and no access to traditional credit who own valuable items to pledge
People needing fast emergency cash ($100-$5,000+) without credit checks or lengthy approval processes
Individuals buying used merchandise or seeking deals on jewelry, electronics, tools, and sporting goods
Those preferring non-recourse lending where they cannot be pursued for loan shortfalls
diagnosis: FirstCash is best for consumers facing immediate cash emergencies who own valuable items and have limited access to traditional credit. The primary caveat is that pawn loans are inherently high-cost borrowing (typically 15-25% monthly interest) and carry the risk of collateral loss; borrowers should only pledge items they can afford to lose or plan to retrieve before loan maturity.
services: Pawn loans secured by personal property (jewelry, electronics, tools, appliances, sporting goods, musical instruments)
Retail sales of used merchandise across multiple product categories
Gold, silver, and precious metals buying at spot prices with immediate cash payment
Layaway purchase plans requiring 10% down payment
Item appraisal and valuation for pawn loan purposes
Store locator and online inventory browsing
Lease-to-own and retail finance payment solutions through subsidiary AFF at 15,000+ merchant locations
Cash payment on the spot for items purchased or pawned
Non-recourse pawn lending (no liability for collateral shortfalls)
meta_description: SuperPawn at 4234 W Northern Ave, Phoenix, AZ. Quick cash loans on jewelry, electronics, and tools.
